title: OfficeCLI Help Schema
description: Capability schema for one (format, element) pair. Consumed by `officecli <format> <op> <element> --help --json`, contract tests, and release-time wiki generation. This is the agent-facing source of truth for what officecli can do; it is updated in the same PR as the implementation.

$schema.description: pointer to this meta file for IDE tooling; ignored at runtime

element.description: element name as used in CLI, e.g. shape, paragraph, cell, chart-series

parent.description: if this element only exists as a child of another, name the parent(s). Omit for top-level elements.

note.description: free-form clarification that does not fit into structured fields (e.g. schema source-of-truth pointer, invariants, caveats)

container.description: set to true for read-only root/container entities (presentation, workbook, document, theme, slidemaster, etc.) that are navigated through but never created or mutated. Contract tests skip Add/Set assertions for containers.

operations.description: which top-level operations accept this element

addParent.description: concrete CLI parent path(s) accepted by Add. Used by help renderer to print accurate `officecli add <file> <parent> --type <element>` usage. Required when the element's positional/stable paths describe the element's own addressable location (e.g. /comments/comment[N], /footnotes/footnote[N]) rather than its Add-time parent. If omitted, the renderer derives parent by dropping the last segment of paths.positional[0].

paths.description: path forms accepted when this element is addressed by index or @id

addressing.description: used when children of this element are addressed by a key attribute (e.g. axis[@role=value]) rather than [N]. Mutually exclusive with plain positional paths.

key.description: name of the keying attribute, e.g. 'role'

keyValues.description: permitted values for the key attribute

properties.description: properties supported on this element. Key = canonical property name.

children.description: declared child element types addressable under this element in CLI paths

parts.description: for the synthetic `raw` element only: enumerates the raw OOXML parts addressable via the `raw` and `raw-set` commands (e.g. /workbook, /Sheet1, /styles). Rendered as a 'Parts' section by the help renderer. Not used by other elements.

name.description: part path as accepted by `officecli raw <file> <part>` (e.g. /workbook, /Sheet1)

desc.description: one-line description of what this part contains

examples.description: element-level command examples (in addition to per-property examples). Used primarily by the synthetic `raw` element.

description.description: element-level description (in addition to per-property descriptions). Used primarily by the synthetic `raw` element.

elementAliases.description: alternate element names that should resolve to this schema (e.g. `paragraph.json` declares ['p'] so that `help docx p` works the same as `help docx paragraph`). Lets path-form abbreviations used in /body/p[N], /Sheet1/col[B], etc. line up with the help index.

appliesWhen.description: conditional applicability. Keys are dotted paths into sibling/ancestor state (e.g. 'chartType', 'role', 'parent.chartType'). Values are arrays of admissible values. ALL keys must match (AND).

aliases.description: legacy/lenient names accepted on input, normalized to the canonical key on output. Array form = plain alias list. Object form = alias → canonical mapping (useful when one canonical has multiple aliases pointing to distinct canonical values, e.g. chartType).

values.description: for type=enum, the allowed canonical values

modifiers.description: for enum-like properties with orthogonal modifiers (e.g. chartType + 3d/stacked). Each modifier declares how it composes into the final value and which base values it applies to.

requires.description: other properties on the same element that must be set together with this one for the OOXML result to be well-formed (e.g. opacity requires fill to attach alpha to). Contract tests automatically bundle `requires` entries.

readback.description: expected format of the Get readback value

enforcement.description: strict = contract test failure breaks CI; report = drift only logged. New properties default to strict; historical debt may start as report and migrate.

element.description: name of the child element's schema file (without .json)

pathSegment.description: CLI path segment for this child, e.g. 'series', 'title', 'axis'

cardinality.description: 0..1 = singleton optional (no [N]); 1 = singleton required; 0..n / 1..n = indexed or keyed

key.description: if children are addressed by attribute instead of [N], name of that attribute (e.g. 'role' for axis[@role=value])

keyValues.description: permitted values for the key attribute
